steps.title: Identify the requirement for the claim
steps.content: The second team claims these sounds are primarily for intraspecies communication. For this claim to hold, other caterpillars must actually be present when sounds are produced—communication requires an audience.

steps.title: Test which finding most directly challenges this
steps.content: Choice C reveals that the caterpillars producing these sounds are typically solitary and tested in isolation. If no other caterpillars are present, intraspecies communication is physically impossible, directly destroying the second team's claim. Choices A and D suggest the sounds serve defensive functions instead, while B addresses species that don't produce sounds during attacks. That matches choice C — C is correct.
